message.content:
# Tesla Q4 2025 Earnings Call Transcript - Available Quotes

Based on the search results provided, I can only access **partial quotes and statements** from the Tesla Q4 2025 earnings call held on January 28, 2026. The search results contain limited direct quotes from the webcast and PDF materials. Here are the available statements:

## Executive Remarks

**On Automotive Margins:**
"Automotive margins excluding credits improved sequentially from 15.4 to 17.9%."[1]

"The automotive gross profit was flat sequentially despite 16% lower deliveries primarily due to regional mix as we had proportionately more deliveries in APAC and AMIA."[1]

**On 2026 Strategy:**
"As we look to 2026 with the progress that has been made with autonomy our focus is on ramping production at all our factories. Our biggest constraint globally continues to be on the battery pack front."[1]

**On FSD Transition:**
"Beginning this quarter, we are transitioning fully to a subscription-based model for FSD. Therefore, net additions to this figure will primarily be via subscription model and in the short term will impact automotive margins."[1]

**On Energy Business:**
"On the energy front, we achieved yet another record in terms of gross profit for the quarter and ended the year with nearly 12.8 billion in revenue, a 26.6% year-over-year growth."[1]

**On Gross Margin Achievement:**
"We ended the quarter with over 20.1% [gross margin], something which we haven't achieved for over the last two years. This improvement came despite the impact of lower fixed cost absorption and the impact of tariffs which were in excess of 500 million in Q4."[1]

**On Autonomy Focus:**
"Autonomy software will be the driver for growth from now on and as we aim to maximize the global feed we've been laser focused on cogs from our side to make sure because that is something which we manage."[1]

**On Unsupervised FSD Development:**
"Basically like two goals. One is like learn as much as possible from the fleet with the safety monitors and secondly we laser focused with the engineering team to solve the unsupervised FSD problem. By the end of last year we had a long tail of issues that we were able to churn through."[1]

"I wouldn't say there's anything that like really surprised us because we had a large fleet. We had all the metrics. So, there was not sort of a surprise. It was just continued work to grind down on the long tail of issues and that's what enabled us to launch the unsupervised service in Austin."[1]

## Limitations

The search results do not provide a complete transcript of the earnings call. The full Q&A session with analysts and complete prepared remarks from executives (including the CFO) are not fully captured in these search results. To access the complete transcript with all quotes and statements, you would need to:

- Visit Tesla's investor relations website directly at ir.tesla.com
- Access the full webcast recording on YouTube
- Request the official SEC filing transcript (Form 8-K or earnings call transcript)
